metadata.name: Flanking — Suq'Ata Lancer gives blocker -1/-1, kills blocker in combat
metadata.description: p1 controls Suq'Ata Lancer (2/2, Haste, Flanking). p2 controls Audacious Thief (2/2, no Flanking). p1 attacks with the Lancer targeting p2. p2 blocks with Audacious Thief. CR 702.25a: Flanking triggers during the declare blockers step — 'Whenever a creature without flanking blocks this creature, the blocking creature gets -1/-1 until end of turn.' The trigger goes on the stack; both players pass priority; the trigger resolves, giving Audacious Thief -1/-1 until end of turn, reducing it to 1/1. In the combat damage step, both creatures deal damage simultaneously (CR 510.1): Suq'Ata Lancer (power 2) deals 2 damage to the now-1/1 Audacious Thief; Audacious Thief (power 1 after -1/-1) deals 1 damage to Suq'Ata Lancer. SBA 704.5g: Audacious Thief has toughness 1 and received 2 damage (lethal) — it is destroyed. Suq'Ata Lancer has toughness 2 and received 1 damage (not lethal) — it survives. p2 takes no player damage (Lancer was blocked).
metadata.generation_notes: Suq'Ata Lancer oracle text (MCP verified): Creature — Human Knight, {2}{R}, 2/2, Haste, Flanking. CR 702.25a: 'Flanking is a triggered ability that triggers during the declare blockers step. Flanking means: Whenever this creature becomes blocked by a creature without flanking, the blocking creature gets -1/-1 until end of turn.' Audacious Thief oracle text (Available Cards list): 2/2, has no Flanking — legal blocker for this test and valid flanking trigger target. Audacious Thief's attack trigger ('When Audacious Thief attacks, draw a card, you lose 1 life') does NOT fire here because p2 controls Audacious Thief and it is blocking, not attacking. Turn 4 used to avoid summoning sickness on Suq'Ata Lancer (CR 302.6); however Haste means it could attack turn 1, but turn 4 keeps it consistent with other combat scripts. Flanking trigger fires at declare blockers step: at the moment p2 declares Audacious Thief as a blocker for Suq'Ata Lancer, the Flanking trigger is put on the stack. Both players pass priority; trigger resolves: Audacious Thief gets -1/-1 until end of turn, becoming 1/1. Combat damage is then simultaneous (CR 510.2): Suq'Ata Lancer (power 2) assigns 2 damage to Audacious Thief; Audacious Thief (power 1, post-flanking) assigns 1 damage to Suq'Ata Lancer. SBA 704.5g: Audacious Thief has toughness 1 and 2 damage marked (2 >= 1) → lethal → destroyed, goes to p2 graveyard. Suq'Ata Lancer has toughness 2 and 1 damage marked (1 < 2) → not lethal → survives. p2 life remains 40 (blocked attacker cannot deal player damage, CR 510.1c). At cleanup (CR 514.2), the -1/-1 flanking bonus would expire (Audacious Thief is dead so moot). Damage on Suq'Ata Lancer is also removed at cleanup.

script.step_note: p1 declares Suq'Ata Lancer as an attacker targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Lancer becomes tapped. No attack triggers fire (Flanking triggers on blocking, not attacking). Priority resets to p1.

actions.description: Initial: Suq'Ata Lancer untapped on p1 battlefield; Audacious Thief untapped on p2 battlefield; life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Both creatures are untapped. Turn 4 — no summoning sickness (CR 302.6). Audacious Thief has no Flanking — it is a valid flanking trigger target when it blocks the Lancer.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Suq'Ata Lancer as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Suq'Ata Lancer becomes tapped. CR 508.1k: Suq'Ata Lancer becomes an attacking creature. Flanking does NOT trigger at this point — CR 702.25a specifies it triggers during the declare blockers step when the creature 'becomes blocked by a creature without flanking'.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Suq'Ata Lancer tapped, Audacious Thief untapped, stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Suq'Ata Lancer tapped as part of attack declaration. No triggers fire at this point — Flanking triggers during declare blockers, not declare attackers.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: p2 declares Audacious Thief as a blocker for Suq'Ata Lancer. CR 702.25a: Flanking triggers — Audacious Thief (no Flanking) is blocking Suq'Ata Lancer (Flanking) — one triggered ability is placed on the stack giving Audacious Thief -1/-1 until end of turn.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares Audacious Thief as a blocker for Suq'Ata Lancer. CR 509.1a: Audacious Thief is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Audacious Thief becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Suq'Ata Lancer becomes a blocked creature. CR 702.25a: Suq'Ata Lancer has Flanking; Audacious Thief has no Flanking — the Flanking triggered ability fires, placing one trigger on the stack: 'Audacious Thief gets -1/-1 until end of turn.'

actions.description: After declare_blockers: Flanking trigger on the stack (count 1), both creatures still on battlefield
actions.note: CR 702.25a: One Flanking triggered ability is on the stack. Audacious Thief has no Flanking, so the trigger fires. The trigger will give Audacious Thief -1/-1 until end of turn when it resolves, reducing it from 2/2 to 1/1.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the Flanking trigger (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.25a: Flanking trigger resolves — Audacious Thief gets -1/-1 until end of turn. Audacious Thief is now 1/1 (was 2/2).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Flanking triggered ability resolves. Audacious Thief receives -1/-1 until end of turn, reducing it from 2/2 to 1/1.

actions.description: After Flanking trigger resolves: stack empty, both creatures still on battlefield, Audacious Thief now effectively 1/1
actions.note: Flanking trigger has resolved. Audacious Thief has a -1/-1 until-end-of-turn effect applied, making it 1/1. Both creatures remain on the battlefield entering combat damage.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to CombatDamage step.

script.step_note: Combat damage turn-based action (CR 510.1): Suq'Ata Lancer (power 2, blocked) assigns 2 damage to Audacious Thief; Audacious Thief (power 1 after -1/-1) assigns 1 damage to Suq'Ata Lancer. Damage is simultaneous (CR 510.2). SBA 704.5g: Audacious Thief has toughness 1 and 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. Suq'Ata Lancer has toughness 2 and 1 damage — not lethal — survives.

results.description: Audacious Thief has toughness 1 (2/2 base minus -1/-1 from Flanking = 1/1) and 2 damage marked (from Suq'Ata Lancer's power 2) — 2 >= 1 — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Audacious Thief's effective toughness is 1 (2 base - 1 from Flanking) and it has 2 damage marked — lethal damage — it is destroyed and moves to p2's graveyard. Suq'Ata Lancer has toughness 2 and only 1 damage marked — not lethal — Lancer survives. SBAs are applied as a batch (CR 704.3).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage resolves and SBAs have been applied. Stack is empty — advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: After combat damage and SBAs: p2 at 40 life (Lancer was blocked), Suq'Ata Lancer on p1 battlefield, Audacious Thief in p2 graveyard
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Suq'Ata Lancer was blocked — it assigned all 2 damage to the blocker (Audacious Thief), not to p2. p2 life remains 40. Audacious Thief's toughness was reduced to 1 by Flanking; it received 2 damage (power 2 from Lancer) — lethal — SBA 704.5g destroyed it, moved to p2 graveyard. Suq'Ata Lancer received 1 damage (Audacious Thief's power 1 after -1/-1) against its 2 toughness — not lethal — Lancer remains on battlefield (tapped, with 1 damage counter removed at cleanup).
